lede: A look at June 16 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Anna Cathcart and Urby Emanuelson, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: 2019–2020 Hong Kong protests
events.desc: Upwards of 2,000,000 people participate in the 2019–20 Hong Kong protests, the largest in Hong Kong's history.

events.title: Revolutions of 1989
events.desc: Revolutions of 1989: Imre Nagy, the former Hungarian prime minister, is reburied in Budapest following the collapse of Communism in Hungary.

events.title: Vostok 6
events.desc: Soviet Space Program: Vostok 6 mission: Cosmonaut Valentina Tereshkova becomes the first woman in space.

events.title: National Industrial Recovery Act of 1933
events.desc: The National Industrial Recovery Act is passed in the United States, allowing businesses to avoid antitrust prosecution if they establish voluntary wage, price, and working condition regulations on an industry-wide basis.

events.title: IBM
events.desc: IBM founded as the Computing-Tabulating-Recording Company in Endicott, New York.

events.title: James Joyce
events.desc: Irish author James Joyce begins a relationship with Nora Barnacle and subsequently uses the date to set the actions for his novel Ulysses; this date is now traditionally called "Bloomsday".

events.title: Victoria Hall disaster
events.desc: The Victoria Hall theatre panic in Sunderland, England, kills 183 children.

events.title: London Working Men's Association
events.desc: The formation of the London Working Men's Association gives rise to the Chartist Movement.

events.title: 1819 Rann of Kutch earthquake
events.desc: A major earthquake strikes the Kutch district of western India, killing over 1,543 people and raising a 6-metre-high (20 ft), 6-kilometre-wide (3.7 mi), ridge, extending for at least 80 kilometres (50 mi), that was known as the Allah Bund ("Dam of God").

events.title: Plymouth Company
events.desc: The Plymouth Company granted a land patent to Thomas Purchase, the first settler of Pejepscot, Maine, settling at the site of Fort Andross.

deaths.text: Kim Woodburn, English television personality and expert cleaner
Jo Cox, English political activist and MP
Ignatius Kutu Acheampong, Ghanaian general and politician, 6th Head of state of Ghana
Sir Francis Bernard, 1st Baronet, English lawyer and politician, Governor of the Province of Massachusetts Bay

observances.text: Christian feast days: Aureus of Mainz (and his sister Justina)
